verdict_reasoning: EREG is canonically a secreted EGF-family ligand with no intrinsic membrane anchor. However, like many EGF-family proligands, it exists as a single-pass TM precursor (pro-EREG) that undergoes regulated ectodomain shedding by sheddases (TACE/ADAM17, ADAM10). The TM-anchored precursor stage is documented and contributes to juxtacrine EGFR/ERBB4 signaling before proteolytic release into the soluble shed form. The soluble shed ectodomain is the dominant biological actor in circulation and in most experimental contexts, but the membrane-anchored precursor does transiently populate the cell surface during regulated processing. This dual-stage lifecycle (transient TM precursor + dominant shed ligand) qualifies as contextual via dual_localization.
